meaning: adj.希腊的,希腊式的n.希腊学家
examples: The East also had a more deeply rooted unity in the Greek culture of the numerous Greek and Near Eastern cities that Rome had inherited from earlier Grecian empires.

The most knock-out dress was the draped Grecian gown with open sides that recalled the work of Madame Gres.
Elena Ambrosiadou, the Greek founder of the Cyprus-based hedge fund Ikos, first laid eyes on the Maltese Falcon as she watched it glide by the Grecian island of Delos.
